claims.text: U.S. data center demand is projected to reach 75.8 GW in 2026.
claims.context: Projected U.S. data center electricity demand in 2026

claims.text: Data center grid-power demand rose 22% in 2025 and is expected to nearly triple by 2030.
claims.context: Year-on-year growth rate of data center grid power demand

claims.text: Annual compliance costs for electricity market agents under the EU AI Act are estimated between less than one million to nearly 200 million euros per country.
claims.context: Maximum estimated annual EU AI Act compliance cost per country for electricity market agents

claims.text: A profit growth of 10% through AI utilization is needed to offset the highest compliance costs of the EU AI Act.
claims.context: AI profit growth required to offset EU AI Act compliance costs

claims.text: The global smart grid cybersecurity market was valued at USD 7.2 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 25.65 billion by 2033.
claims.context: Smart grid cybersecurity market size in 2025

claims.text: Only 10% of large enterprises will have a fully mature Zero Trust program by 2026, up from less than 1% in 2023.
claims.context: Zero Trust maturity adoption rate among large enterprises

claims.text: Germany, France, and the Netherlands curtailed a record 3.9 TWh of renewable energy in 2025.
claims.context: Record renewable energy curtailment in three European countries

claims.text: Solar curtailment in Germany surged 97% year-on-year to 1,389 GWh in 2024.
claims.context: Year-on-year increase in solar curtailment in Germany

claims.text: Germany is preparing a second round of Carbon Contracts for Difference with EUR 6 billion available for 2026.
claims.context: Budget for Germany's 2026 CCfD auction round

claims.text: Germany has set aside roughly EUR 50 billion for the overall CCfD scheme.
claims.context: Total budget for Germany's Carbon Contracts for Difference program

claims.text: Germany's EUR 500 billion infrastructure fund allocates EUR 100 billion to the Climate and Transformation Fund for energy transition.
claims.context: Climate and energy transition allocation within Germany's infrastructure fund

claims.text: Between 2026 and 2029, the German government intends to allocate nearly EUR 60 billion per year via the special fund.
claims.context: Annual spending from Germany's infrastructure fund

claims.text: 104 GW of coal and natural gas capacity is scheduled for retirement by 2030.
